     The Numbers Business Report

Never Let Go (2024)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $10,306,106Details
International Box Office $11,454,607
Worldwide Box Office $21,760,713
Further financial details...

Synopsis

As an Evil takes over the world beyond their front doorstep, the only protection for a mother and her twin sons is their house and their family’s protective bond. Needing to stay connected at all times—even tethering themselves with ropes—they cling to one another, urging each other to never let go. But when one of the boys questions if the evil is real, the ties that bind them together are severed, triggering a terrifying fight for survival.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$4,449,065 (43.2% of total gross)
Legs:2.32 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:47.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,667 opening theaters/2,667 max. theaters, 2.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $10,898,406

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 20th, 2024 (Wide) by Lionsgate
International Releases: September 18th, 2024 (Wide) (Philippines)
September 18th, 2024 (Wide) (Indonesia)
September 19th, 2024 (Wide) (Oman)
September 19th, 2024 (Wide) (Kuwait)
September 19th, 2024 (Wide) (Iraq)
Video Release: October 11th, 2024 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong violent content and grisly images.
(Rating bulletin 2849 (cert #54905), 8/28/2024)
Running Time: 101 minutes
Filming location (country): Canada
Subgenre: Survival Horror
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: 21 Laps Entertainment, Lionsgate, Media Capital Technologies, HalleHolly
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

As an Evil takes over the world beyond their front doorstep, the only protection for a mother and her twin sons is their house and their family’s protective bond. Needing to stay connected at all times—even tethering themselves with ropes—they cling to one another, urging each other to never let go. But when one of the boys questions if the evil is real, the ties that bind them together are severed, triggering a terrifying fight for survival.
